new_posts.title: GitHub Copilot for Eclipse is open source
new_posts.summary: Following our previous updates, GitHub Copilot for Eclipse is open source, with the code available on GitHub under the MIT license. This marks an important milestone for GitHub Copilot in…

new_posts.title: Issue fields are now in public preview for all organizations
new_posts.summary: Issue fields are now available in public preview to all GitHub organizations on github.com and GitHub Enterprise Cloud with data residency. When you define typed metadata like Priority, Effort, or&

new_posts.title: Copilot usage metrics reports now use GitHub-owned download URLs
new_posts.summary: As previously announced, the download URLs for Copilot usage metrics reports have migrated from Azure Front Door domains to a stable, GitHub-owned custom domain. This change improves URL stability

new_posts.title: Updates to available models in Copilot on web
new_posts.summary: We have updated our available model selection for Copilot Chat on the web to deliver more consistent, high-quality responses. What’s changed While model choice is valuable, we are limiting th

new_posts.title: Auto model selection now routes based on your task in VS Code
new_posts.summary: GitHub Copilot auto model selection now routes to the best model for your task, using utilization and model health metrics for a high quality, reliable, and token-efficient experience. How it…

new_posts.title: Semantic issue search in Copilot Chat
new_posts.summary: You can use natural language in GitHub Copilot Chat on web to quickly find, group, and analyze issues, with context-aware results powered by a new semantic issues index. What’s new…

new_posts.title: Easily apply Copilot code review feedback with Copilot cloud agent
new_posts.summary: Copilot code review’s previous Implement suggestion button has now been renamed to Fix with Copilot and updated to support a UI dialog for more control over how suggestions are applied.…

new_posts.title: Upcoming deprecation of Python 3.9 for Dependabot
new_posts.summary: On June 23, 2026, Dependabot will no longer support Python version 3.9, which has reached its end-of-life. If you continue to use Python 3.9, there’s a risk that Dependabot will…

new_posts.title: Gemini 3.5 Flash is generally available for GitHub Copilot
new_posts.summary: Gemini 3.5 Flash, Google’s latest Flash-tier model, is now rolling out on GitHub Copilot. In our early testing, Gemini 3.5 Flash delivers near-Pro coding quality at Flash-tier speed and cost&

new_posts.title: Expanded OIDC support for Dependabot and code scanning
new_posts.summary: Dependabot and code scanning now support OpenID Connect (OIDC) authentication for private registries configured at the organization level for two additional registries: Cloudsmith and Google Artifa

new_posts.title: Take your local GitHub sessions anywhere
new_posts.summary:
Kick off work in VS Code or the CLI, finish it from your phone. Remote control for GitHub Copilot sessions is now generally available on github.com and GitHub Mobile.

The post

new_posts.title: Building a general-purpose accessibility agent—and what we learned in the process
new_posts.summary:
Learn about the experimental general-purpose accessibility agent that GitHub is piloting.

The post

new_posts.title: Dungeons & Desktops: Building a procedurally generated roguelike with GitHub Copilot CLI
new_posts.summary:
Learn how one Hubber used GitHub Copilot CLI to build an extension that turns any codebase into a unique, roguelike dungeon.

The post
